pattern: Two nodes are reachable exactly when they share a connected component, so the unreachable pairs are all node pairs minus the pairs that fall inside each component.
hints: Reachability partitions the nodes into connected components; a pair is unreachable iff the two nodes are in different components.
Find the size of every connected component, for example with union-find or a traversal.
As you reveal each component of size c, every earlier node (count so far) pairs with all c of its nodes across the divide; sum c * (already-seen) to total the unreachable pairs.

edge_inputs.description: fully connected triangle, no unreachable pairs
single node, no pairs at all
no edges means every pair is unreachable
duplicate-free edges forming two equal halves

stress.description: random sparse graph producing several components
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    m = rng.randrange(0, n)
    seen = set()
    edges = []
    attempts = 0
    while len(edges) < m and attempts < m * 4:
        attempts += 1
        a = rng.randrange(0, n)
        b = rng.randrange(0, n)
        if a == b:
            continue
        key = (min(a, b), max(a, b))
        if key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(key)
        edges.append([a, b])
    return [n, edges]
